prompt: Interior of a warm cafe, viewed from a slight overhead angle. At a small central table, one person sits alone with a coffee cup, looking around the room — clearly engaged, not lonely, part of the social fabric. Three other tables are visible: at the first, five people in animated conversation; at the second, four people sharing food; at the third, a group of six clustered together. The central person has a visible social connection to each of these tables — small eye-contact gestures, a raised hand of acknowledgment. But every single person in every group is surrounded by more people than the central figure. The central figure has connections; each connection is embedded in a denser cluster. The overall feeling is not loneliness but a quiet mathematical inevitability — the most connected position is always held by someone else. Shot from two meters above table height, 35mm equivalent, natural window light, warm golden tones. No text, no labels, no phone screens with readable content. Negative: text, labels, phone screens with content, readable signs, sad/lonely atmosphere, darkness, isolation signifiers, diagrams
visual_logic: Each terrace tilts upward left-to-right; the overall hillside descends left-to-right. Both directions are simultaneously visible and irrefutable — local trend and global trend contradict each other.
